game_info.title: HexaDungeon
game_info.concept:
"Blanket recommendation for this one." - Pocket Tactics (5 stars)

App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/hoplite/id782438457

================================================================================
Full Description:
================================================================================

"Blanket recommendation for this one." - Pocket Tactics (5 stars)

"It's very simple to learn, but there's a huge amount of depth to the game" - Touch Arcade (5 stars)


Hoplite is a turn-based strategy game focusing on tactical movement around small maps.

This game is designed to be hard, yet fair.  You'll need to think before you act if you hope to win.  Every move counts!

Quest Mode:
• Make strategical choices to upgrade your abilities as you dive deeper into the underworld.
• Earn achievements and unlock access to new upgrades.
• Procedurally generated levels give a new experience every play.
• Challenge your friends and see how you rank on the leaderboard.

Challenge mode:
• Jump straight into the action with a randomized set of upgrades.
• Try to beat the daily challenge or play predetermined challenges at two difficulty levels.
game_info.description:
HexaDungeon is a tactical turn-based strategy game played on a hexagonal grid. You play as a gladiator descending into the underworld. Your goal in each level is to reach the portal to the next depth while surviving enemy attacks.

Movement and combat are distinct: moving into an enemy attacks them, while moving to an empty tile simply changes your position. Enemies telegraph their attacks—watch out for red "Danger Zones" indicating where enemies will strike next turn. One wrong step can be fatal!
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Keys: Move the selection cursor around the grid
- Space: Confirm action (Move to tile or Attack enemy at cursor)
- Z: Wait / Skip turn (Tactical wait to lure enemies)
- Shift: Hold to view detailed enemy info and attack ranges
- Enter: Start Game / Next Level
- R: Restart Game (from Game Over)
- ESC: Pause / Resume

TEST_1.test_description: Verify basic movement and danger avoidance mechanics.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test controller will move the player towards the exit using A* pathfinding. It will specifically check the "danger map" to avoid stepping into tiles threatened by enemies in the next turn.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should survive for at least 15 seconds or complete the first level, demonstrating that danger avoidance logic is functional.

TEST_2.test_description: Verify combat aggression and objective seeking.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The test controller will prioritize attacking enemies adjacent to the optimal path. If the path to the portal is blocked by an enemy, it will choose to attack rather than wait.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player should successfully kill at least one enemy and reach the portal, advancing the level counter.

TEST_3.test_description: Verify wait mechanic and enemy AI response.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The test controller will use the 'Z' key (Wait) when enemies are just out of range but approaching, verifying that enemies move closer, allowing the player to strike first.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Enemies should close the distance during player wait turns, and the player should survive the interaction.
